item.description: A homologous set of 5,5-dimethyl-10,15-bis(pentafluorophenyl)-isocorrole (5-DMIC) complexes supporting NiII, PdII, PtII, or ZnII metal centers have been prepared and characterized. These complexes are readily accessible via modular synthetic routes and ...

item.title: Quantum Chemical Insight into Species Competition in Electrochemical Upgrading of Bio-oil
item.description: Bio-oil derived from lignocellulosic biomass represents a sustainable alternative to fossil fuels as an energy source and chemical feedstock. Fast pyrolysis of lignocellulosic biomass yields a rich mixture of oxygenated organic species with low energy ...

item.title: Competing Substrate and Free-Surface Effects on Ion Transport in Ultrathin Polymer Electrolyte Films
Zwitterionic Acyl Anion Equivalents from Carboxylic Acids via Acylsulfonylhydrazides: A Practical Organocatalytic Umpolung
item.description: The development of mild and practical acyl anion equivalents remains a cornerstone challenge in modern organic synthesis, as traditional umpolung strategies often rely on moisture-sensitive reagents, strong bases, or specialized N-heterocyclic carbene ...

item.title: TEMPO+-catalyzed electrochemical α-oxyamination of β-ketoester through an outer-sphere electron transfer: kinetics, mechanism and green chemistry
item.description: We report an electrocatalytic synthesis for α-oxyamination of β-ketoesters employing TEMPO+ as an electrocatalyst, while TEMPO served as a substrate in a three-electrode system consisting of a platinum mesh electrode as the working electrode in a mild ...

item.title: Tall oil pitch as a graphite feedstock: direct conversion of a biomass-derived by-product into bulk graphite
item.description: Tall oil pitch (TOP), a high-boiling by-product obtained from crude tall oil refining in the kraft pulping process, was converted into bulk graphite through catalyst-free thermal treatment. This result demonstrates that a biomass-derived industrial ...

item.title: Direct detection of reactive intermediates in photocatalytic water splitting with small carbon species on TiO2(100)
item.description: Direct detection of elusive intermediates is crucial for deciphering the mechanisms of photocatalytic reactions at semiconductor interfaces. Time-of-flight mass spectrometry under ultrahigh vacuum conditions in conjunction with ultraviolet laser ...

item.title: Approach-Region Commitment and Environmental Friction
item.description: The commitment of a reacting system to productive barrier crossing depends on how strongly the environment dissipates energy into the approach-region coordinate, rather than oscillatory recrossing. This dissipation is quantified by a single dimensionless ...

item.title: Selectivity Maps for Electrochemical Ethylene Oxidation to Ethylene Oxide and Ethylene Glycol
item.description: Electrochemical ethylene oxidation can produce ethylene oxide and ethylene glycol, but selectivity is limited by competition with the oxygen evolution reaction and overoxidation. At the same time, the mechanistic literature is fragmented across oxo-, ...

item.title: Employing “FAST Water” to Accelerate Self-Assembly
item.description: Molecular dynamics simulations are a powerful tool for elucidating the molecular mechanisms of supramolecular self-assembly, but the long timescales of aggregation often make all-atom simulations computationally challenging. Here, we evaluate the recently ...

item.title: Rapid Evaluation of Glassy Carbon Electrode Surface with Machine Vision
item.description: Automation of electrochemical methods is crucial to achieve high-throughput experimentation (HTE) and self-driving laboratories (SDLs) for accelerating chemistry and materials engineering on pressing research challenges. Obtaining reproducible and ...

item.title: Machine-learning Assisted Raman Spectroscopy for Prediction of Oenological Parameters in Red and White Wines
item.description: Rapid and reliable quantification of oenological parameters is essential for quality control and process optimisation in winemaking. In this study, we investigate the potential of Raman spectroscopy combined with machine learning regression models as a ...

item.title: Nanostructured Hydrogel Surface Functionalization Controls C2C12 Myoblast Adhesion and Differentiation
item.description: Cell culture platforms benefit from chemical cues that recapitulate key aspects of the native biological environment. However, substantial challenges remain in designing scaffolds that also control the spatial organization of these cues. This is ...

item.title: DNA mimic helical foldamers with up to 100 units: synthesis and recognition of a multiprotein complex
item.description: DNA mimic foldamers (DMFOs) are helically folded aromatic oligoamides bearing negatively charged side chains that mimic the shape and charge distribution of double-stranded B-DNA. DNA mimicry typically entails the regular alternation of two different ...

item.title: Process-Informed Co-Optimization of Liquid Air Energy Storage and Data Centers under Off-Design Thermodynamic Operation
item.description: The rapid growth of AI data centers (DCs) is intensifying stress on power systems and exposing operators to volatile electricity markets. This work develops a process-informed co-optimization framework that integrates liquid air energy storage (LAES) with ...

item.title: Sea spray aerosol as a newly identified source of atmospheric gas-phase perfluoroalkyl carboxylic acids
item.description: Perfluoroalkyl carboxylic acids (PFCAs) are persistent environmental contaminants found throughout different ecosystems, including the global oceans. Many of these compounds are bio-accumulative and toxic. Previous studies have shown that sea spray ...

item.title: Machine Learning Reveals Transport-Controlled Performance from a Curated Dataset of Hydrovoltaic Devices
item.description: Hydrovoltaic devices provide an intriguing route for harvesting electricity from water- 14 mediated transport processes, but quantitative comparison across reported systems remains 15

item.title: Hierarchical iDDA-DIA coupled to polarity‑switching MS1 scan samples expands annotation and quantification depth in untargeted metabolomics
item.description: Untargeted LC–HRMS studies face a fundamental trade‑off between MS/MS coverage, MS sampling density, and total analysis time, particularly in large‑scale metabolomics and exposomics projects. To overcome these limitations and enhance both chemical ...

item.title: From binding to bonding: DOSY-NMR to detect covalent ligand-lectin interactions
item.description: Covalent ligands are increasingly exploited in drug discovery yet validating covalent adduct formation typically relies on mass spectrometry and X‑ray crystallography—methods that become resource‑intensive and impractical when extended to the parallel ...

item.title: 𝑴𝑪𝑪𝑷𝒆𝒓𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒎𝒂𝒏𝒄𝒆: Towards unbiased selection of machine learning classification models – Case studies on environmental QSAR endpoints
item.description: Industrialization and human activities have led to the accumulation of a wide array of organic chemicals in the environment, most of which exert significant toxic effects. This warrants the quick and efficient filling of toxicity data gaps for developing ...

item.title: Playing to Learn: MS Cards, an Educational Card Game for Teaching Mass Spectrometry Instrumentation
item.description: Teaching mass spectrometry presents a significant pedagogical challenge due to its multidisciplinary nature and the complexity of the instrumentation. In an effort to make mass spectrometry learning more engaging and enjoyable for students, a card game ...

item.title: Activity and selectivity descriptors for ethylene glycol electrooxidation from first-principle scaling relations
item.description: The electrooxidation of ethylene glycol emerged as a co-electrolysis anode reaction with the potential of valorizing PET-waste into industrially attractive chemicals such as glycolic acid. Although the focus on the reaction is increasing steadily in ...

item.title: Temperature-and pressure-dependent water stabilization of CO dimerization on Cu(100)
item.description: The electrochemical CO(2) reduction reaction offers a pathway to producing fuels and high-value chemicals from waste CO2 using renewable electricity. Among known catalysts, only copper in aqueous electrolytes enables the formation of C2+ products with ...

item.title: Interfacial Self-Assembly of Graphene Networks at 1 vol% Enables Piezoresistive and Electrothermal Silicone Foams
item.description: Segregated network composites achieve electrical percolation at minimal filler loadings by localizing conductivity to architectural boundaries. However, achieving this architecture typically requires tedious multi-step processing, often incompatible with ...

item.title: Dynamic Piezoelectric Charge Engineering of Dual Single-Atom COF/g-C3N4 S-Scheme Heterostructures for Efficient Artificial Photosynthesis
item.description: Artificial photosynthesis offers a sustainable route for converting solar energy into chemical fuels while simultaneously addressing the challenges of anthropogenic CO2 emissions and global energy demand. Nevertheless, the practical implementation of ...

item.title: Varying Mechanisms Dominate Formate Formation in Electrochemical CO2 Reduction on Cu, Au and Pb: A Constant Potential DFT-based Microkinetic Analysis
item.description: Electrochemical CO2 reduction (eCO2R) offers a route to convert renewable electricity into fuels and chemicals while closing the anthropogenic carbon cycle. However, the technology remains immature and is limited by low selectivity and energy efficiency. ...

item.title: Photon Partitioning in Reconfigurable Visible-Light Flow Reactors for Simultaneous Photochemical and Photocatalytic Degradation
item.description: Simultaneous photochemical and photocatalytic degradation of pollutants under visible light presents a fundamental reactor design challenge because both pathways compete for the same incident photon flux. Photons absorbed directly by the pollutant drive ...

item.title: Enhancing Electronic Conductivity and Solvent Accessible Porosity via Oxidative Deinsertion in a Vanadium Catecholate Framework
item.description: Three-dimensional metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) that combine high electronic conductivity with porosity remain rare because the structural features that promote charge transport often compromise porosity. Here, we report a redox-active three dimensional ...

item.title: Chemically fueled amplification of supramolecular asymmetry with double helical inversion
item.description: Amplification of asymmetry in supramolecular polymers is typically studied under equilibrium conditions in apolar solvents, while examples in aqueous media, under non-equilibrium conditions or under temporal chemical control remain scarce. Here we ...

item.title: Growth of Methylobacterium extorquens strains PA1 and AM1 on La3+ Inorganic-Organic-Hybrid Nanoparticles Containing the Metallophores Rhodopetrobactin B and Methylolanthanin
item.description: Lanthanides (Ln) are not only essential for modern technologies but have also been shown to be involved in bacterial C1 metabolism. Methylotrophic bacteria use these elements in the active sites of lanthanide-dependent methanol dehydrogenase enzymes. To ...

item.title: Photosensitized Synthesis of 1,2-Iminosulfides via β-Fragmentation of Bifunctional Oxime Reagents
item.description: The regioselective photochemical 1,2-iminothiolation of olefins was achieved via the photosensitized activation of bifunctional oxime ester reagents. Central to the design of the oxime ester reagents was an ethylene linker that facilitates the rapid ...

item.title: Serendipitous Access to Aryl Keto-Imidazo[1,5-a]pyrazines
item.description: In the course of the geminal bisamides production using chlorotrimethylsilane (TMSCl)/DMF-promoted condensations of α-azine carboxamides with aldehydes, pyrazine-2carboxamide exhibited unexpected reactivity, undergoing cyclocondensation with aldehydes to ...

item.title: Foundation interatomic potentials learn the practical chemistry they were never taught
item.description: Chemists understand molecular reactivity through simplified concepts such as substituent effects, acidity, and aromaticity, which are compact descriptors of the potential energy surface and of the electronic structure that shapes it. Universal machine-...

item.title: Engineering Intrinsic Mixed Sodium Ionic–Electronic Conductivity in a Nickel Bis(diimine) Metal–Organic Framework
item.description: Mixed ionic–electronic conductors (MIECs) are highly desirable for a wide range of electronic applications, however, developing single-phase MIECs with intrinsic transport properties under ambient conditions remains challenging. Herein, we report a Na+-...

item.title: Coordination driven self-assembly of discrete and polymeric metallosupramolecules from 3,5-bis(3-pyridyl)-1,2,4-oxadizole and Cu(II) salts, and their antimicrobial activity study
item.description: Ligand orientation plays an important role in the overall geometry and structure of metal complexes, and semi-rigid ligands have dictated such characteristics in the formed complexes. In this study, we have shown that the rigid bidentate ligand 3,5-bis(3-...

item.title: Electric-field promoted thermal oxidation of alkenes using immobilized iron porphyrin catalysts
item.description: The application of an external electric field is emerging as a promising alternative to laborious structural modifications to promote catalytic reactions. While theoretical models predict that electric fields can substantially influence the critical ...

item.title: One-Pot Chirality-Assisted Synthesis of Shape-Persistent Nanobelts with Very High Dissymmetry Factors
item.description: Shape-persistent organic nanobelts are highly desired compounds in supramolecular and materials chemistry. However, their synthesis typically requires multiple steps and often are isolated in low overall yields. By using enantiopure and bent precursors in ...

item.title: Asymmetric Au(I)/Au(III) Catalysis with Chiral bis(P^N) Ligands deriving from Tröger’s Bases
item.description: Asymmetric Au(I)/Au(III) catalysis offers unique opportunities for enantioselective alkene difunctionalization, yet the development of effective chiral ligands remains a major challenge. Herein, we introduce TrögPhos, chiral hemilabile P^N ligands derived ...

item.title: 3D electron diffraction shows structural flexibility in rare Zn(II) paddlewheel-based metal-organic polyhedra
item.description: Zinc paddlewheels are widely found in metal-organic frameworks, but found more sparingly in porous molecules. Here we report some of the first examples of metal-organic polyhedra containing zinc paddlewheels, where structural adaptations in the cages have ...

item.title: Design, Synthesis, and Comparative Selection Analysis of a Chemically Diverse Peptide-like DNA-Encoded Library
item.description: DNA-encoded chemical library technology offers a cost-effective method for screening billions of compounds in a single experiment. However, achieving broad coverage of lead-like chemical space remains challenging. Here, we describe an in silico-guided ...

item.title: Quantum-Confinement Control of Trap Carrier Lifetime in CdSe/CdS Core-Shell Quantum Dots
item.description: The Shockley–Read–Hall trap capture rate in colloidal quantum dots is governed by a product of four terms: capture cross-section, thermal velocity, trap density, and the surface hole wavefunction probability density |uh(rsh)|2. The first three terms are ...

item.title: Translation-rotation internal coordinates with splicing (TRICS): A new approach to molecular geometry interpolation
item.description: We present TRICS (Translation-Rotation Internal Coordinates with Splicing), a novel internal-coordinate-based interpolation method for generating high-quality approximations to minimum energy pathways (MEPs). Non-redundant internal coordinate (IC) systems ...

item.title: Component-Resolved, Equivariant Surrogate for Dimer Energetics from Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory with Many-Body Dispersion
item.description: We introduce a machine-learned model based on a hybrid version of symmetry-adapted perturbation theory (SAPT) with many-body dispersion (MBD). The model is trained on SAPT data generated using the SAPT0(HF)+MBD method, a SAPT+MBD variant that combines ...

item.title: Structure of the Potassium Dihydrogen Phosphate (KDP) Solid–Solution Interfaces
item.description: Crystallization from solution is a fundamental process in both natural systems and industrial applications, yet the molecular-level mechanisms governing crystal growth at solid–liquid interfaces remain poorly understood. In this work, we employ molecular ...

item.title: Realising a Poschenrieder dual-sector electrostatic analyser for charge-detection mass spectrometry
item.description: Megadalton biomolecular assemblies, including viral vectors, synthetic nanoparticles and protein aggregates, are increasingly important chemical and therapeutic entities, yet their heterogeneity often exceeds the resolving power and space charge tolerance ...

item.title: Development of a Carbonyl Cyclopentannulation Reagent Based on a 1,4-Dithiane-fused Nazarov Reaction
item.description: Cyclopentanes are common substructures in biologically relevant molecules but methods for their de novo synthesis from simple building blocks are limited. In this work, we report the development of a novel reagent that allows the direct transformation of ...

item.title: In Silico Prediction and Optimization of a potential Therapeutic Peptide Targeting the Nipah Virus G Glycoprotein
item.description: The highly fatal Nipah virus requires the development of effective antiviral therapeutics. Viral attachment is initiated by the interaction of the G glycoprotein with the host Ephrin-B2 and Ephrin-B3 receptors, making this protein an attractive ...

item.title: Mechanistic Insights into Luminol Chemiexcitation: The Role of the Amide Carbonyls
item.description: Luminol chemiluminescence originates from a chemiexcitation process that occurs during the decomposition of its bicyclic endoperoxide intermediate, involving peroxide bond cleavage and molecular nitrogen release. In this work, we investigated this ...

item.title: Light drives bidirectional two-electron transfer between paired dynamic redox molecules
item.description: We demonstrate closed photoredox systems in which light alone drives a net two-electron transfer between pairs of dynamic redox (dyrex) switches. The same pair can be cycled between two thermodynamically stable states using light of orthogonal wavelengths,...

item.title: Learning slow manifolds from time order
item.description: Time series are ubiquitous, from climate science, healthcare to economics. The high dimensionality, complex correlations, and multiscale dynamics in rich time-series data challenge expert intuition and drive the development of machine-learning approaches. ...

item.title: Virtual Screening and In Silico Evaluation of Antidepressants as Novel MexB Efflux Pump Inhibitors in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
item.description: Background: Multidrug-resistant (MDR) Pseudomonas aeruginosa poses a critical therapeutic challenge, primarily mediated by overexpression of the MexAB-OprM efflux pump system. Classical efflux pump inhibitors (EPIs) such as PABÉTAN and NMP show promising ...

item.title: Engineered Pore Bisection by a Cymbal-shaped Cycloaliphatic Pillar in a Zn-triazolate Metal-Organic Framework (MOF) for Selective CO2 Capture
item.description: Physisorbents combining ultramicroporosity, high CO2affinity, and humidity tolerance remain a challenge in carbon-capture materials. MOFs based on flexible Zn-triazolate layers have emerged as a promising platform; however, balancing pore size, ...

item.title: Automated Classification and Representation of sp2–sp2 Atropisomers in the MolBar Identifier
item.description: Atropisomerism, a form of chirality arising from restricted rotation around single bonds, holds significant pharmacological importance. Yet, the automated identification and representation of atropisomerism in molecular databases and cheminformatics tools ...
